This is a regime filter signal that instructs traders to avoid entry entirely during ranging market conditions (flat MA, price oscillating in tight bands, low volatility) rather than providing actionable entry/exit rules; position_size_multiplier of 0.0 confirms no trades should be executed.

Signal cannot be operationalized: range boundaries and their definitions are marked AUTHOR_SILENT, making it impossible to construct testable price comparison rules without explicit threshold values or indicator-based range definitions.

Trade mean reversion when breakout attempts fail: go long near range lows after upside breakout failure, go short near range highs after downside breakout failure, in range-bound markets with low ADX.

entry_long.description: Price near lower range boundary
Price rebounds back inside range after failed breakout down
Market is range-bound (low directional strength)

entry_short.description: Price near upper range boundary
Price falls back inside range after failed breakout up
Market is range-bound (low directional strength)

exit_long.description: Exit long when price breaks below moving average (reversal)

exit_short.description: Exit short when price breaks above moving average (reversal)

rules.reason: Trade mean reversion when breakout attempts fail: go long near range lows after upside breakout failure, go short near range highs after downside breakout failure, in range-bound markets with low ADX.

reason: Trade mean-reversion at range boundaries after failed breakouts; entry when price reverts inside range in low-ADX environment, exit on momentum reversal or MA cross.

entry_long.description: Price near lower range boundary
Low directional momentum confirms ranging market
Price has fallen back inside range after breakout attempt

entry_short.description: Price near upper range boundary
Low directional momentum confirms ranging market
Price has fallen back inside range after breakout attempt

exit_long.description: Exit on trend reversal or MA crossover
Exit on overbought conditions

exit_short.description: Exit on trend reversal or MA crossover
Exit on oversold conditions

rules.reason: Trade mean-reversion at range boundaries after failed breakouts; entry when price reverts inside range in low-ADX environment, exit on momentum reversal or MA cross.

reason: Buy when price reaches lower range (price_pos_20 < 0.3), sell when it reaches upper range (price_pos_20 > 0.7); exits trigger on reversal to opposite range boundary.

rules.reason: Buy when price reaches lower range (price_pos_20 < 0.3), sell when it reaches upper range (price_pos_20 > 0.7); exits trigger on reversal to opposite range boundary.

reason: This is a rejection filter that prevents entries rather than generating actionable buy/sell signals; it requires counting support tests across multiple bars which cannot be expressed in single-bar conditional logic without custom helper columns.

Identifies mean-reversion opportunities when price moves away from SMA-20 with elevated volatility (ATR spike), triggering longs below MA (uptrend snapback) and shorts above MA (downtrend snapback), exiting on MA crossback.

rules.reason: Identifies mean-reversion opportunities when price moves away from SMA-20 with elevated volatility (ATR spike), triggering longs below MA (uptrend snapback) and shorts above MA (downtrend snapback), exiting on MA crossback.

reason: This is a regime identification signal that advises avoiding directional trades in range-bound markets rather than providing actionable entry/exit rules; it functions as a filter to suppress trades, not generate them.
Enter long when MA slopes upward (detected via ADX confirmation), price stays above MA, and trend regime is active; exit when price closes below MA.

rules.reason: Enter long when MA slopes upward (detected via ADX confirmation), price stays above MA, and trend regime is active; exit when price closes below MA.

reason: This signal is a risk filter/confluence rule that prevents entry rather than defines tradeable entry conditions; it requires pattern detection (successive lower bounce highs) that cannot be expressed as simple point-in-time indicator comparisons without custom bounce-detection logic.

Enter long when MA slopes upward (price > MA) and exit when MA crosses below price; enter short when MA slopes downward (price < MA) and exit when MA crosses above price, reverting to trend-following exits since author provided no exit conditions.

rules.reason: Enter long when MA slopes upward (price > MA) and exit when MA crosses below price; enter short when MA slopes downward (price < MA) and exit when MA crosses above price, reverting to trend-following exits since author provided no exit conditions.

reason: Price crossing MA with slope confirmation triggers long/short entries; price crossing back triggers exits in a trending regime.

rules.reason: Price crossing MA with slope confirmation triggers long/short entries; price crossing back triggers exits in a trending regime.

reason: Price crossing above/below moving average triggers trend reversals; exits occur on counter-crossovers in a trending regime.

rules.reason: Price crossing above/below moving average triggers trend reversals; exits occur on counter-crossovers in a trending regime.

reason: Buy on retracement to MA support in uptrends; sell on rally to MA resistance in downtrends; exit on MA crossover reversals.

rules.reason: Buy on retracement to MA support in uptrends; sell on rally to MA resistance in downtrends; exit on MA crossover reversals.

reason: Enter longs when price crosses above the 200 SMA after it turns up; enter shorts when price stays below 200 SMA during downtrend; exit on reversal crosses.

rules.reason: Enter longs when price crosses above the 200 SMA after it turns up; enter shorts when price stays below 200 SMA during downtrend; exit on reversal crosses.

reason: Entry triggers when price crosses below a moving average (using sma_50 as default intermediate period) and momentum turns bearish; exit on reversal above the MA line within a trending regime.

notes.implementation_requirement: Engine must pre-calculate ORH (opening_range_high) and ORL (opening_range_low) from the first N bars where N = opening_range_duration / bar_size.
